content.full_text:
Farn Polrrrcar PRACrrcBs C oMMrssroN
P.O. Box 807 . 428 J Street o Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
September 79,2007
James D. Sanders
224F,.21s Street
Merced, CA 95340
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-01.200
Dear Mr. Sanders:
This is in response to your request for advice under the conflict of interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").I Since you are not inquiring about a
specific governmental decisjon before you at this time, we are treating your request as
one for informal assistance.2 (Regulation 18329(bX8).)
QUESTION
Does your position with the Merced community Action Network (MCAN)
prevent you from serving as a member of the Merced City Council if you are elected to
that position in November?
CONCLUSION
The Act does not prevent any person, based on their employer and occupation,
from serving as an elected official in California. However, theie may be instanies when
you must disqualify yourself from participating in decisions that may affect your
employer. The analysis below will provide you with guidance.
t Govemment Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Tifle 2, sections 18109-
18997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.
2Pursuant to Regulation 13329(c)(3), informal assistalce does not confer immunitv.


File No. I-01-200
Page2
F'ACTS
You are a candidate for the Merced City Council in the November 6,2OOt
election. You are employed by the Merced Community Action Networlq a non-profit
corporation, as its president and C.E.O.
MCAN has a contractwith the City of Merced to provide graffrti abatement,
neighborhood watch and video production services. You are concerned, and questions
have been raised, that this arrangement is a potential conflict that would prevent you from
serving as a city council member if you were elected in November.
ANALYSIS
"No public official at any level of state or local government shall make,
partrcipate in making or in any way attempt to use his official position to influence a
governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a financial
interest." (Section 87100.) An economic interest includes:
"(a) Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or
indirect investment worth two thousand dollars ($2,000) or more.
"(b) Any real property in which the public official has a direct or
indirect interest worth two thousand dollars ($2,000) or more.
"(") Any source of income, except gifts or loans by a commercial
lending institution made in the regular source of business on terms
available to the public without regard to official status, aggregating five
hundred dollars ($500) or more in value provided or promised to, received
by, the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.
"(d) Any business entity in which the public official is a director,
officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of management.
"(") Any donor of, or any intermediary or agent for a donor of, a
gift or gifts aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value
provided to, received by, or promised to the public official within 12
months prior to the time when the decision is made...3
"For purposes of this section, indirect investment or interest means
any investment or interest owned by the spouse or dependent child of a
public official, by an agent on behalf of a public official, or by a business
3 The amount of the value of gifts specified by subdivision 87103(e) is adjusted biermially by the
Commission to reflect changes in tlre Consumer Price Index, rounded to the nearest ten tlollars ($10).
Effective January 7,2001, the disqualification threshold for donors of gifts is $320. @egulation 18940.2.)


File No. I-01-200
Page3
entity or trust in which the official, the official's agents, spouse, and
dependent children own directly, indirectly, or beneficially a 10 percent
interest or greater."
(Section 87 1 03 (a)-(e).)
If you are successful in your election to the Merced City Council, you must
disqualify yourself from participating in any decision that would have a reasonably
foreseeable materialfinancial ffict on your economic interests, including the Merced
Community Action Network.
Since you are not as yet a public official and do not have a specific governmental
decision before you, we can only provide, in general terms, how a decision might have a
material fnancial effect on MCAN.
The Commission distinguishes between decisions that have a"direct" effect on
your financial interests from decisions that "indirectly" affect those interests. For
example, a decision to renew the contract with MCAN would directly affect MCAN
since it would be the subject of the decision, whereas a decision by the city to change the
amount of funding to be dedicated to its graffiti removal program may have an indiiect
effect on MCAN. A decision that has a direct effect on a financial interest is deemed to
have a material financial effect on the interest and disqualification on that particular
decision is required. (Regulations 18704.1; 18705.3 )
A public official would also be disqualified from participating if the decision
would have an indirect material financial effect on the interest. The standard for
determining indirect material financial effect for a non-profit organi zation depends on the
financial size of the organization. For example, a decision having a $10,000 effect on a
local community non-profit organization would be material as to that organizalion, yet
the same decision having a $10,000 effect on a very large national non-profit
organization would not necessarily have a material financial effect on that organization.
(Regulation 18705.3(b)(1).) Without knowing the financial size of MCAN or a specific
decision pending that might affect MCAN, we cannot provide specific advice on this
issue. If you are elected and a decision affecting MCAN is before you, it is strongly
suggested that you contact us at that time so we can appropriately advise you.
Please note, the Fair Political Practices Commission is authorized only to render
advice on public officials' obligations under the Act. However, there may well be other
bodies of law bearing on your concern. Government Code Section 1090 bars public
officials from entering into certain contractual relationships with governmental entities.
We urge you to contact the Attorney General's office for advice in this regard.
I hope this answers your question or provides you with the assistance you sought.
If you have further questions, please feel free to contact me at (866) 275-3772. Ihave


File No. I-01-200
Page 4
enclosed a pamphlet titled"Can I Vote? An Overview of Public Officials' Obligations
Under the Political Reform Act's Conflict-of-Interest Rules." This publication may
provide assistance to you as well.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By:
Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
Enclosures
LMlrETljt

prevent you from serving as a member of the Merced City Council if you are elected to
that position in November?
